name: Malawi

colors.symbolism: the blood of the martyrs of African independence; also the rising sun on the black band, representing the dawn of hope and freedom for Africa

description: Three equal horizontal stripes — black, red and green — with a red rising sun, comprising a half-disc and 31 short straight rays, centred on the upper black stripe (the rays emerging from the lower edge of the half-disc).
history: Adopted on 6 July 1964, the day Malawi (formerly Nyasaland) gained independence from the United Kingdom. The Pan-African black-red-green palette was taken from the flag of the Malawi Congress Party, which had led the independence movement. The rising sun is a fresh element representing the dawn of independence in Africa. From 2010 to 2012, under President Bingu wa Mutharika, the flag was briefly redesigned (red on top, full white sun on a black middle band) to reflect the country's economic progress; this 'Bingu flag' was unpopular and the original 1964 design was restored on 28 May 2012.

features.symbolism: black for the African people, red for the blood of African martyrs, green for the country's evergreen nature; the Pan-African colours of the Universal Negro Improvement Association (Marcus Garvey)

features.role: rising sun on the black band
features.symbolism: the dawn of hope and freedom for Africa; the rising sun specifically marks Malawi's independence as a fresh beginning
